summary: Mid‑80s arena audience tape with a hollow/boomy sound, solid playing and a standout performance of “The Dew”.
review: The recording is an audience capture that suffers from a hollow, slightly boomy mids‑80s arena ambience, but it remains listenable. Musically the show is competent – nothing earth‑shattering – with highlights such as “The Dew”, a lively Wheel and a nice outro jam on Foolish, while songs like Terrapin and Mighty Quinn feel listless. Overall opinions are mixed, reflecting both the decent musicianship and the modest audio fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews note an average‑to‑good performance (70% weight) but consistently describe a fair, hollow audience recording (30% weight), yielding a combined rating just above the midpoint.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hollow, boomy mid‑80s arena sound; some reviewers call it tinny or “concave”. Minor feedback issues on Victim and overall lack of nuance, but no severe dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is well‑spaced with patient pacing; songs transition smoothly though the hollow recording makes them blend together, resulting in a solid but not spectacular overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgets major chunks of lyrics during Terrapin, otherwise guitar work is competent.
band_member_comments.Bob: Birthday shout‑out noted; vocal inflections on The Dew praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums receive strong praise; the Drum/Space segment described as erratic but overall energy is good.
